title: sedum schizolepis
intro: Sedum schizolepis, also known as the 'Lungwort Sedum', is a succulent plant species that belongs to the Crassulaceae family, typically found in mountainous regions of China. The plant contains a range of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Sedum schizolepis has been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health issues, including coughs, bronchitis, and fever, due to its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Sedum schizolepis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to leverage its health benefits, including improved respiratory function and immune system support.
